channel.title: Stockwatch - Key Events
channel.description: Real-time corporate announcements, deals, and financial results.

item.title: [ESABINDIA] Esab India: Kevin Johnson Resigns from Board, Committees
item.description:
Esab India Ltd announced the resignation of Kevin Johnson from its Board of Directors, effective May 1, 2026. This follows ESAB Holdings Limited's withdrawal of his nomination as a Nominee Director. Johnson cited personal reasons for his resignation. Consequently, he also ceases to be a member of the Audit Committee, Nomination and Remuneration Committee, Stakeholders’ Relationship Committee, Corporate Social Responsibility Committee, and Chairman of the Risk Management Committee.

  • Kevin Johnson resigned as Director of Esab India, effective May 1, 2026.
  • ESAB Holdings Limited withdrew his nomination as Nominee Director.
  • He also stepped down from five key board committees.
  • Resignation was attributed to personal reasons.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: ESAB INDIA LTD. | Category: Management Change

item.title: [SHREECEM] Shree Cement's appeal against ₹3.6 Lac penalty dismissed
item.description:
Shree Cement Limited's appeal against a demand order for wrong availment of Cenvat Credit, which included a penalty of ₹3,59,831, has been dismissed by the Commissioner (Appeals), CE & CGST, Jodhpur. The dismissal was for technical reasons, thereby reinstating the penalty. The company received the order on April 29, 2026, and is currently reviewing it with legal advisors to evaluate next steps, including a potential appeal before the Tribunal. The company has stated that this order will have no major financial impact.

  • Appeal against a ₹3,59,831 penalty dismissed by Commissioner (Appeals).
  • Dismissal for technical reasons reinstates penalty for Cenvat Credit.
  • Company is reviewing the order and considering an appeal to the Tribunal.
  • Management states the order has no major financial impact on the company.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: SHREE CEMENT LTD. | Category: Tax & Penalty

item.title: [KDLL] KD Leisures Ltd to change name to Vedic Ayurveda Ltd
item.description:
KD Leisures Ltd's Board of Directors approved changing the company name to Vedic Ayurveda Limited and consequential alterations to its constitutional documents. The board also regularized Ms. Kanchan Yadav as an Independent Non-Executive Director, Ms. Deepika Awasthi as an Executive Director, and Ms. Anubha Kumari Sinha as a Non-Executive Director, all effective April 30, 2026. Additionally, the alteration in the object clause of the Memorandum of Association was approved. An Extra-Ordinary General Meeting (EGM) will be convened to seek shareholder approval for these changes, with M/s Amit Saxena & Associates appointed as scrutinizer for the e-voting process.

  • Company name to change from KD Leisures Ltd to Vedic Ayurveda Ltd.
  • Board approved regularization of three directors: Kanchan Yadav, Deepika Awasthi, and Anubha Kumari Sinha.
  • Alteration in the object clause of the Memorandum of Association was approved.
  • An EGM will be held to obtain shareholder approval for the resolutions.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: KD Leisures Ltd | Category: Corporate Action

item.title: [QUEST] Ampvolts Board to meet May 8 for Q4/FY26 results
item.description:
Ampvolts Ltd (formerly Quest Softech (India) Limited) has announced that its Board of Directors will hold a meeting on Friday, May 8, 2026. The main purpose of this meeting is to consider and approve the audited financial results for the quarter and the full financial year ended March 31, 2026. In accordance with the company's code of conduct, the trading window for designated persons, which began on April 1, 2026, will remain closed until 48 hours after the results are publicly disclosed on May 8, 2026.

  • Board meeting scheduled for May 8, 2026.
  • To consider and approve Q4 and FY26 audited financial results.
  • Trading window closed from April 1, 2026.
  • Trading window reopens 48 hours after results are public.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Quest Softech (India) Ltd | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [SMARTWORKS] Smartworks: Monitoring Agency Report for Q4 FY26 shows no IPO deviation
item.description:
Smartworks Coworking Spaces Ltd has submitted its Monitoring Agency Report for the quarter ended March 31, 2026, in compliance with SEBI regulations. The report, issued by CARE Ratings Limited and reviewed by the company's Audit Committee, confirms that there was "No deviation" from the objects of the IPO issue. This disclosure pertains to the utilization of the ₹445.00 crore IPO proceeds, ensuring transparency and adherence to the stated objectives.

  • Smartworks submitted its Q4 FY26 Monitoring Agency Report.
  • CARE Ratings Limited confirmed no deviation from IPO objects.
  • Report covers utilization of ₹445 crore IPO proceeds.
  • Audit Committee reviewed and took the report on record.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Smartworks Coworking Spaces Ltd | Category: Regulatory

item.title: [DAL] Dynamic Archistructures Board to meet May 25 for Q4/FY26 results
item.description:
Dynamic Archistructures Ltd has announced that its Board of Directors will convene a meeting on Monday, May 25, 2026. The primary agenda for this meeting is to consider and approve the Audited Financial Results and Financial Statements for the Fourth Quarter and the full Financial Year ended March 31, 2026. The meeting will take place at the company's Registered Office. This disclosure is made in accordance with Regulation 29 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015.

  • Board meeting scheduled for May 25, 2026.
  • To consider Q4 and FY26 audited financial results.
  • Meeting held at the company's Registered Office.
  • Announcement made as per SEBI Regulation 29.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Dynamic Archistructures Ltd | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [COCHINSHIP] Cochin Shipyard: Head of Internal Audit Mary Ranjit Abraham Retires
item.description:
Cochin Shipyard Limited announced the retirement of Smt. Mary Ranjit Abraham, Deputy General Manager (Internal Audit) and Head of Internal Audit, due to superannuation. Her retirement from the company's services was effective April 30, 2026. This change in senior management was disclosed in accordance with Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015.

  • Smt. Mary Ranjit Abraham retired from CSL.
  • She was Deputy General Manager and Head of Internal Audit.
  • Her superannuation was effective April 30, 2026.
  • This constitutes a change in senior management.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Cochin Shipyard Ltd | Category: Management Change

item.title: [ESCORTSFIN] Invigorated Business Consulting Board approves FY26 results
item.description:
Invigorated Business Consulting Ltd's Board of Directors convened on April 30, 2026, and approved the audited financial results for the quarter and financial year ended March 31, 2026. The company confirmed that the Auditor's Report on these results was enclosed and carried an unmodified opinion, in compliance with Regulations 30 and 33 of the SEBI Listing Regulations.

  • Board meeting held on April 30, 2026, from 4:00 P.M. to 5:00 P.M.
  • Audited financial results for Q4 and FY26 approved by the Board.
  • Auditor's Report on the financial results has an unmodified opinion.
  • Filing made pursuant to SEBI Listing Regulations 30 and 33.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: ESCORTS FINANCE LTD. | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [PFC] PFC Consulting incorporates 5 wholly-owned SPVs for transmission projects
item.description:
PFC Consulting Limited (PFCCL), a wholly-owned subsidiary of Power Finance Corporation Limited (PFC), has incorporated five new wholly-owned Special Purpose Vehicle (SPV) companies. PFCCL has been nominated as the Bid Process Coordinator (BPC) by the Ministry of Power for selecting developers for Independent Transmission Projects (ITPs) through tariff-based competitive bidding. The newly incorporated SPVs are JAM KHAMBHALIYA REZ TRANSMISSION LIMITED, LAKADIA A POWER TRANSMISSION LIMITED, LAKADIA C POWER TRANSMISSION LIMITED, ANANTHPURAM TRANSMISSION LIMITED, and KRISHNAGIRI REZ TRANSMISSION LIMITED. These SPVs will undertake preparatory activities for the transmission projects, such as land acquisition and forest clearance, and will subsequently be transferred to the successful bidders selected through the competitive bidding process.

  • PFC Consulting incorporated five new wholly-owned SPVs.
  • SPVs are for Independent Transmission Projects (ITPs).
  • PFCCL is the Bid Process Coordinator for these projects.
  • SPVs will be transferred to successful bidders post-bidding.
  • Projects integrate power from REZs in Gujarat and other regions.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: POWER FINANCE CORPORATION LTD. | Category: Subsidiary

item.title: [UNIVCABLES] Universal Cables: VP Production Mahesh Singh resigns
item.description:
Universal Cables Ltd announced the resignation of Mr. Mahesh Singh, Vice-President Production, a Senior Management Personnel. His resignation is effective from the close of business hours on April 30, 2026. The company stated that the reason for his departure is personal. Mr. Singh, who served the company for six and a half years, expressed gratitude for the opportunity and committed to ensuring a smooth transition during his remaining tenure, offering to assist with training his replacement and documenting current projects.

  • Universal Cables' VP Production, Mahesh Singh, has resigned.
  • His resignation is effective from April 30, 2026.
  • The reason for his departure is cited as personal.
  • Singh served the company for six and a half years.
  • He will assist with a smooth transition of duties.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: UNIVERSAL CABLES LTD. | Category: Management Change
